subject: [PATCH v5 1/4] openat2: new OPENAT2_REGULAR flag support

reviews.author: Dorjoy Chowdhury
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dorjoy Chowdhury agreed that the issues found by Claude were valid and suggested specific modifications to fix them: adding OPENAT2_REGULAR to the flags cleared in do_dentry_open, and updating VALID_OPEN_FLAGS to VALID_OPENAT2_FLAGS in fs/open.c and fs/fcntl.c
reviews.raw_body:
Good catches! I guess for issue 1 I need to modify the line in
do_dentry_open implementation to
f->f_flags &= ~(O_CREAT | O_EXCL | O_NOCTTY | O_TRUNC | OPENAT2_REGULAR);
right?
And for issue 2, I should change the VALID_OPEN_FLAGS to
VALID_OPENAT2_FLAGS in both build_open_flags in fs/open.c and in
fcntl_init in fs/fcntl.c, correct?

Regards,
Dorjoy

2026-03-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a new flag called OPENAT2_REGULAR to the openat2() function, which restricts file opens to regular files only and returns an error for non-regular files. The patch also includes filesystem-specific checks in ceph, gfs2, nfs, and cifs to ensure that resources are properly cleaned up when returning errors. However, two issues were found: the flag leaks into f_flags and a BUILD_BUG_ON check was not updated. These issues need to be fixed before the patch can be applied.
